# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-engine-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default

- name: cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    databaseVersion: POSTGRES_9_6
    settings:
      tier: db-n1-standard-1
      dataDiskType: PD_SSD
      dataDiskSizeGb: 10

- name: cloud-storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.bucket
  properties:
    location: US
    storageClass: REGIONAL

- name: load-balancer
  type: compute.v1.urlMap
  properties:
    defaultService: $(ref.backend-service.selfLink)

- name: backend-service
  type: compute.v1.backendService
  properties:
    backends:
    - group: $(ref.instance-group.selfLink)
    healthChecks:
    - $(ref.http-basic-check.selfLink)

- name: http-basic-check
  type: compute.v1.httpHealthCheck
  properties:
    requestPath: "/"

- name: instance-group
  type: compute.v1.instanceGroup
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    namedPorts:
    - name: http
      port: 80
    instances:
    - $(ref.compute-engine-instance.selfLink)
